input:
May rang no bells. The dogs of hesychasm breezed through the hall past cameras and lasers, flinging music from the stand. We hauled a hope chest across a swelling, tipping pier, every heartbeat a plank creaking above the churn. Feelers folded up in Casimir spaces beneath the cannonade of her seashell eyes, her brow gliding through the pastel morning. Have we passed Utnapishtim's station? Where was salvation? Lord Jesus Christ, have mercy on me, a sinner.

Time heals all wounds, exposed, pussing, ethyl-doused, faces hazy hovering, setting protective aggression to bed. "What news?" says the beach mouse, grateful for the shoulder check, tucked into the forbidden desert, hoping it will come out be benign.
